name: mml-tasks

metadata.name: mml-tasks
classifiers: Topic :: Software Development :: Libraries :: Application Frameworks
Topic :: Software Development :: Version Control :: Git
metadata.summary: This is the MML tasks plugin, providing dataset and task implementation for a bunch of datasets.

documentation:
# MML Tasks plugin

This plugin provides a wide range of datasets and tasks, ready to be imported.

> In order to create tasks 'mml' might automatically download data. It is your responsibility to take care of the 
> individual licensing regulations of such data and comply with them. See the respective task creators for more details.

## Install

```commandline
pip install mml-tasks
```

Some of the tasks require the [kaggle API](https://github.com/Kaggle/kaggle-api), which requires some authentication 
to be set up. Based on the documentation this can be done via a `kaggle.json` file at a location depending on your OS, 
or as the MML internal solution add your [credentials](https://github.com/Kaggle/kaggle-api#api-credentials) to the 
`mml.env` file.

```commandline
export KAGGLE_USERNAME=your_kaggle_username
export KAGGLE_KEY=your_kaggle_api_key
```

## Usage

Tasks can be created with the `create` mode as usual. Furthermore, mml-data provides some convenience functionality 
to show and filter for available tasks. Type `mml-tasks --help` for more details.

## Outdated URLs

Dataset download links might become deprecated over time. Please report any outdated urls via the issue tracker and we 
will try to fix those.

Creates dataset

```
param dset_name

Name of the dataset

string

return

Path to dataset directory

rtype

PosixPath
```

Creates task for suncolondb classification

```
param dset_path

Path to dataset

type dset_path

Path
```

functions.name: mml_tasks.creators.classification.suncolondb.get_case_paths
documentation:
Returns list with paths of cases in subdirectories as well as whether they are positive or negative

```
param data_parts

list of directories where cases are saved

type data_parts

list of strings

param dset_path

path to dataset directory

type dset_path

PosixPath

param ispos

Whether the data_parts contain the positive samples

type ispos

bool

return

List of paths to all the cases in dataset, along with whether they are positive or negative

rtype

list of [PosixPath, bool]
```

functions.name: mml_tasks.creators.classification.suncolondb.load_annotations
documentation:
Load annotations and stores them in dict

```
param dset_path

path to directory

type dset_path

PosixPath

return

Dict contating one dict per video containing class values per frame

rtype

Dict of dicts of ints
```

functions.name: mml_tasks.creators.classification.suncolondb.get_data_iterators
documentation:
Splits the dataset into train and test and generates data iterators

```
param case_paths

list of paths and whether they are from positive or negative samples

type case_paths

list of [PosixPath, bool]

param all_annotations

Dict contating one dict per video containing class values per frame

type all_annotations

Dict of dicts of ints

return

Dict containing train and test sets, containing a list of identifier, path and class

rtype

dict of list of dict
```

functions.name: mml_tasks.cli.get_all_avail_meta_data
documentation: Reads in meta-data of available task creators without actually installing those tasks.

functions.name: mml_tasks.cli.get_all_installed_tasks
documentation: Gathers all installed tasks and clusters by variants thereof.

functions.name: mml_tasks.cli.list_tasks
documentation:
Lists all available tasks with filters applied.

```
param Optional[List[TaskType]] task_types

only show tasks of any given type

param Optional[List[Keyword]] keywords

only show tasks that are marked with all provided keywords

param bool installed

only show tasks that are installed (via create mode)

param bool variants

instead of the default columns, show installed variants of tasks

return

prints a table with columns ['name', 'dataset', 'type', 'installed', 'license'] and filtered
available tasks, if variants is True, the columns will be ['name', 'installed', 'variants']
```

functions.name: mml_tasks.cli.cli
documentation: Parse arguments to mml-data and call the list_tasks function to print task information.
